Load data

{% raw %} 
data = pd.read_csv('data/Lipophilicity_processed.csv')
smiles_list = data['processed_smiles'].values
y = data['exp'].values
y_range = (y.min(), y.max()+0.5)
{% endraw %}

Helper functions

{% raw %}
{% endraw %}

MolecularGraph class

{% raw %}

encode_onehot[source]

encode_onehot(value:int, choices)

Creates a one-hot encoding with an extra category for uncommon values. :param value: The value for which the encoding should be one. :param choices: A list of possible values. :return: A one-hot encoding of the :code:value in a list of length :code:len(choices) + 1. If :code:value is not in :code:choices, then the final element in the encoding is 1.

{% endraw %} {% raw %}
{% endraw %} {% raw %}

AtomFeaturizer()

{% endraw %} {% raw %}

atom = normalize_mol(smiles_list[0]).GetAtoms()[0]

print(featurizer.NODE_DIM)

{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
atom_features = featurizer.encode(atom)

{% endraw %} {% raw %}

class BondFeaturizer[source]

BondFeaturizer()

{% endraw %} {% raw %}
{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
bond_featurizer = BondFeaturizer()
bond_featurizer.BOND_DIM

12
{% endraw %} {% raw %}

class MolecularGraph[source]

MolecularGraph(smiles:str, target:array)

Parent class for Molecular graphs

Converts a Chem.Mol object into a molecular graph representation

Arguments: 

smiles : str
    A SMILES representing a molecular structure

Attributes: 

mol : Chem.Mol
    `Chem.Mol` object generated from `smiles`

n_nodes : int
    Number of atoms (nodes) in the graph

n_edges : int
    Number of bonds (edges) in the graph

node_features : numpy.array
    An array with node features

edge_features : numpy.array
    An array with edge features
{% endraw %} {% raw %}
{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
graph = MolecularGraph(smiles_list[0],y[0]).mol_to_graph()
{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
graph.edge_features.size(), graph.node_features.size()

(torch.Size([24, 24, 12]), torch.Size([24, 39]))
{% endraw %} {% raw %}

class MolecularDataset[source]

MolecularDataset(df:DataFrame, smiles_col:str, targets_col:str)

A generic class to handle tabular (e.g. dataframes) data for QSAR tasks.

Parameters 

df : DataFrame
    A `pandas.DataFrame` object with the data used for the modeling task.

targets_col : str
    A string representing the column of `df` with the target variable.

smiles_col : str
    A string representing the column of `df` with SMILES.

Attributes 

df : `DataFrame`
    A `pandas.DataFrame` object with the data used for the modeling task.

cols : array-like
    An array of columns in `df`

targets_col : str
    A string representing the column of `df` with the target variable.

smiles_col : str
    A string representing the column of `df` with SMILES.

smiles : array-like
    An array of SMILES of the molecules in `df`.

y : array-like
    An array of target variable in `df`.

data : array-like
    A n x p matrix with n SMILES and p y values.
{% endraw %} {% raw %}
{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
tabular_dataset = MolecularDataset(data,smiles_col='processed_smiles',targets_col='exp')
tabular_dataset[0:5],tabular_dataset.y[0:5]

(array([['Cn1c(CN2CCN(c3ccc(Cl)cc3)CC2)nc2ccccc21', 3.54],
        ['COc1cc(OC)c(S(=O)(=O)N2c3ccccc3CCC2C)cc1NC(=O)CSCC(=O)O', -1.18],
        ['COC(=O)[C@H](c1ccccc1Cl)N1CCc2sccc2C1', 3.69],
        ['O=C(NC1Cc2ccccc2N(C[C@@H](O)CO)C1=O)c1cc2cc(Cl)sc2[nH]1', 3.37],
        ['Cc1cccc(C[C@H](NC(=O)c2cc(C(C)(C)C)nn2C)C(=O)NCC#N)c1', 3.1]],
       dtype=object),
 array([ 3.54, -1.18,  3.69,  3.37,  3.1 ]))
{% endraw %} {% raw %}

save_df[source]

save_df(df, fname:str)

Save DataFrame to file

{% endraw %} {% raw %}

class MolecularGraphDataset[source]

MolecularGraphDataset(df:DataFrame, smiles_col:str, targets_col:str, task_id:str=None) :: MolecularDataset

Class to create a dataset for MolecularGraphDataset modeling.

The factory methods from_df and from_csv can be used to directly load data from pandas DataFrames or CSV files.

Instead use static methods MolecularGraphDataset.from_df or MolecularGraphDataset.from_csv for that purpose.

Parameters 

data : DataFrame

    A `pandas.DataFrame` object with the data used for the modeling task.

targets_col : str

    A string representing the column of `data` with the target variable.

smiles_col : str

    A string representing the column of `data` with SMILES.

Attributes 

data : MolecularDataset

    A MolecularDataset instance.

x : array-like (n_samples, n_bits)

    An array with calculated descriptors for the processed dataset.

y : array-like (n_samples, n_classes) or (n_samples, )

    An array with target variable values for each sample.

train : MolecularDataset

    A MolecularDataset for the training set (defined with splitter).

valid : MolecularDataset

    A MolecularDataset for the validation set (defaults to 20% of the data).

splits : List[List, List]

    A list containing indices of samples belonging to training and validation sets.

dtype : str

    A string representing the type of the target variable (`continuous`, `binary`, `multiclass`, `continuous-multioutput` or `multi-label indicatior`).


jobtype : str

    Type of modeling task to perform based on `dtype`.

timestamp : str

    A unique time identifier for the modeling task.

save_dir : Path

    A `Path` object representing a folder where files and outputs will be saved.
{% endraw %} {% raw %}
{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
dset = MolecularGraphDataset.from_df(data, targets_col='exp', smiles_col='processed_smiles', task_id='TESTING_GRAPH')
traindset, validset = dset.train, dset.valid
{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
dset.train.smiles.shape,dset.valid.smiles.shape,dset.smiles.shape, dset.max_nodes

((3358,), (840,), (4198,), 115)
{% endraw %} {% raw %}

molgraph_collate_fn[source]

molgraph_collate_fn(data)

{% endraw %} {% raw %}
{% endraw %} {% raw %}

class MolGraphDataLoader[source]

MolGraphDataLoader(dataset=None, bs=None, num_workers=0, pin_memory=False, timeout=0, batch_size=None, shuffle=False, drop_last=False, indexed=None, n=None, device=None, persistent_workers=False, pin_memory_device='', wif=None, before_iter=None, after_item=None, before_batch=None, after_batch=None, after_iter=None, create_batches=None, create_item=None, create_batch=None, retain=None, get_idxs=None, sample=None, shuffle_fn=None, do_batch=None) :: DataLoader

API compatible with PyTorch DataLoader, with a lot more callbacks and flexibility

{% endraw %} {% raw %}
{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
dls = MolGraphDataLoader.dataloders((traindset, validset), collate_fn=molgraph_collate_fn)
{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
xb,yb = dls.one_batch()
{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
xb[2].size()

torch.Size([32, 42, 42])
{% endraw %} {% raw %}

# valid_dl =  DataLoader(validset, bs=64, shuffle=False, create_batch=molgraph_collate_fn, drop_last=True)
# dls = DataLoaders(train_dl, valid_dl)
{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
xb[0].size(), xb[1].size()

(torch.Size([64, 47, 39]), torch.Size([64, 47, 47, 12]))
{% endraw %}

Kipf & Welling

{% raw %} 
class MessageGCN(Module):
    
    """
    
    Message function from Graph convolutional network as described by Kipf & Welling (https://arxiv.org/pdf/1609.02907.pdf) and refactored as MPNN formalism.

       
    """
    
    def __init__(self, n_node_features:int, hidden_units:int, num_layers:int=2):
        
        """
        
        Arguments:
        
        n_node_features : int
            Number of features of each atom
            
        hidden_units : int
            Number of hidden units in `self.gcn`
            
        output_units : int
            Number of output units
            
        num_layers : int (default = 3)
            Number of fully connected layers
            
        dropout : float (default = 0.15)
            Amount of dropout regularization
        
        
        """
        
        self.message_function = nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(n_node_features,hidden_units),nn.ReLU())#feed_forward(input_dim=n_node_features, num_hidden_layers=num_layers, hidden_dim=hidden_units, output_dim=output_units, dropout=dropout)
        
    
    def forward(self,x):
        node_features, adjacency_matrix, degree_matrix = x
        message = self.message_function(degree_matrix@adjacency_matrix@degree_matrix@node_features)
        return (message, adjacency_matrix, degree_matrix)
{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
class UpdateGCN(Module):
    
    """
    
    Vertex update function from Graph convolutional network as described by Kipf & Welling (https://arxiv.org/pdf/1609.02907.pdf) and refactored as MPNN formalism.

       
    """
    
    def __init__(self, n_node_features:int, hidden_units:int, dropout:float=0.15):
        
        """
        
        Arguments:
        
        n_node_features : int
            Number of features of each atom
            
        hidden_units : int
            Number of hidden units in `self.gcn`
            
        output_units : int
            Number of output units
            
        num_layers : int (default = 3)
            Number of fully connected layers
            
        dropout : float (default = 0.15)
            Amount of dropout regularization
        
        
        """
        
        self.update_function = nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(n_node_features, hidden_units//2), nn.Dropout(dropout), nn.ReLU(), 
                                             nn.Linear(hidden_units//2,hidden_units//4), nn.Dropout(dropout), nn.ReLU())
    
    def forward(self, message):
        node_state = self.update_function(torch.mean(message,1))
        return node_state
{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
class GCNMPNmodel(Module):
    
    """
    
    Prototype Graph convolutional layer as described by Kipf & Welling (https://arxiv.org/pdf/1609.02907.pdf) and refactored as MPNN formalism.

       
    """
    
    def __init__(self, n_node_features:int, hidden_units:int, num_outputs:int=1, dropout:float=0.15, y_range=None):
        
        """
        
        Arguments:
        
        n_node_features : int
            Number of features of each atom
            
        hidden_units : int
            Number of hidden units in `self.gcn`
            
        output_units : int
            Number of output units
            
        num_layers : int (default = 3)
            Number of fully connected layers
            
        dropout : float (default = 0.15)
            Amount of dropout regularization
        
        
        """
        self.message_function = nn.Sequential(MessageGCN(n_node_features, hidden_units, dropout),
                                              MessageGCN(hidden_units, hidden_units, dropout),
                                             MessageGCN(hidden_units, hidden_units,dropout))
        
        self.update_function = UpdateGCN(hidden_units, hidden_units, dropout)
        self.readout_function = nn.Linear(hidden_units//4, num_outputs)
        
        
        if y_range:
            self.sigmoid = SigmoidRange(*y_range)
        
    
    def forward(self, x):
        node_features, edge_features, adjacency_matrix, degree_matrix = x # Get input features 
        message = self.message_function((node_features, adjacency_matrix, degree_matrix))
        node_state = self.update_function(message[0])
        out = self.sigmoid(self.readout_function(node_state))
        return out
{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
learn = Learner(dls, model=GCNMPNmodel(39, 500, 1, y_range=y_range, dropout=0.1), loss_func=MSELossFlat(),metrics=[R2Score(), rmse])
learn.summary()
